summary: Verify pinout against schematic (SD card-detect conflict, PSRAM CS, USB host polarity)
description: The learn guide (local adafruit-fruit-jam.pdf, pp.12-30) has at least one internal conflict: SD pin listing gives GPIO34 for BOTH SD_SCK and SD_CARD_DETECT. Verify against the schematic (guide Downloads section, p.309): (1) real card-detect pin; (2) PSRAM CS pin (not stated in pinout pages); (3) USB host D+=GPIO1/D-=GPIO2 order and CH334F hub topology (2 USB-A + third port on 2x16 header); (4) GPIO11 5V-gate polarity/behavior for USB host power; (5) RP2350B silicon revision on actual hardware (guide says A2 stepping -> E9 erratum applies; note pizero shipped rev 3 where Pico-PIO-USB's E9 workaround compiles OUT - chip_version <= 2 gate - which changes hot-replug behavior, see PIZERO-11b). Known-good from guide: HSTX DVI CKn/p=12/13 D0=14/15 D1=16/17 D2=18/19; I2S DIN=24 MCLK=25 BCLK=26 WS=27 IRQ=23 RESET=22 (shared with ESP32-C6 reset); SD SPI MOSI=35 MISO=36 CS=39, SDIO extra 37/38; buttons 0/4/5; NeoPixels 32; LED 29 active-low (shared with IR); Stemma I2C0 SDA=20 SCL=21.

summary: Decision: toolchain - PlatformIO/earlephilhower (prior ports) vs bare Pico SDK
description: All three prior ports used PlatformIO + earlephilhower arduino-pico. Known costs there: build_unflags=-Os mandatory (core appends -Os after your flags, last-wins; 43b COCO-30); PICO_NO_HARDWARE is defined so __not_in_flash_func silently no-ops in C files (must use .time_critical section attrs and verify with nm - pizero hdmi-audio-notes); no LTO in shipped gcc. Pico SDK pros: pico_hdmi (HSTX+audio prior art) is SDK-based, HSTX support is first-class, LTO possible, no -Os trap; cons: lose Arduino USB host convenience wrappers (Adafruit TinyUSB), new build territory. Fruit Jam has official earlephilhower BSP support (guide p.196). Decide, then scaffold: blink + serial hello on the board. Deliverable includes deploy flow (UF2 drag or picotool).

summary: Clock plan: validate 252 MHz sys clock serving HSTX 480p + PIO-USB + emulation headroom
description: Hypothesis from research (2026-07-02): 252 MHz sys clock serves everything. Evidence: (a) PIO-USB '120/240 MHz only' was a myth - dividers derive from clk_sys, 252/48=5.25 exact in PIO 16.8 fractional divider, HW-confirmed on pizero at 252 MHz (PIZERO-44, commit 457f0ad); (b) HSTX 480p wants 25.2 MHz pixel / 252 MHz TMDS bit clock -> clk_hstx 126 MHz; pico_hdmi runs 480p at sys=126 or 252-with-div-2; (c) pizero needed vreg 1.25V at 252 (PIZERO-45) - test what Fruit Jam needs. Verify: how clk_hstx is sourced/divided when sys=252 (aux mux options: clk_sys, pll_sys, pll_usb); whether HSTX + PIO-USB + SD + emulation are all stable at 252/1.25V; measure headroom vs the ~2.4x real-time the pizero achieves. Do NOT hand-edit PLL_SYS_KHZ (breaks QSPI clkdiv - amoled lesson); use set_sys_clock_khz().

summary: HSTX DVI bring-up: 640x480p60, 320x240 RGB565 framebuffer hardware-doubled
description: First board where HSTX (hardware TMDS serializer, GPIO12-19) drives the video - no PIO bit-bang, near-zero CPU for scanout. Evaluate: fliperama86/pico_hdmi (Unlicense, v0.0.7, scanline-callback API, ~6us/callback budget, double-buffered DMA, includes audio data islands for later FRUITJAM-14) vs pico-examples dvi_out_hstx_encoder (minimal, video-only). Target: 640x480p60 CEA timing, 320x240 RGB565 internal framebuffer scanned out 2x (pizero-proven scale: CoCo 256x192 centered in 320x240 -> 512x384 on screen). Consumer model per porting-rp2350.md matrix: self-clocked scanout + SRAM budget -> static framebuffer + pointer swap, presentation on core 1. Acceptance: stable test pattern on a monitor over the DVI port at true 60 Hz.

summary: USB host keyboard behind the CH334F hub (CFG_TUH_HUB - untested in any prior port)
description: PIO-USB host on D+=GPIO1/D-=GPIO2 feeding an onboard CH334F 3-port hub (both USB-A jacks are hub ports) - so TinyUSB CFG_TUH_HUB=1 is MANDATORY and is new territory: no prior port ever enabled hub support (pizero has no CFG_TUH_HUB flag). Also: drive GPIO11 to enable host 5V. Sys clock per FRUITJAM-03 (252 MHz divides exactly for PIO-USB). Known constraints carried from pizero: USB 1.1 full-speed only (high-speed-only keyboards won't enumerate; simple wired boards + 2.4GHz dongles work); hot-replug broken on rev-3 silicon (TX SM pins D+/D- at J after enumeration, unplug shows as ~180/s flood of byte-identical HID reports; fix designed - detect N identical reports then pio_usb_host_stop/restart - but never implemented, see FRUITJAM-17); workaround = cold-boot with keyboard attached. Open question: does the always-attached CH334F hub change enumeration/replug behavior (hub stays enumerated; keyboard plugs into hub port)? That may actually IMPROVE hot-plug vs the pizero. Acceptance: HID keyboard reports arriving with keyboard on either USB-A port.

summary: microSD bring-up: SPI + FatFS, ROMs readable
description: SPI mode first (MOSI=35, MISO=36, CS=39, SCK + card-detect per FRUITJAM-01), ~12.5 MHz like pizero. Carry the lessons: cold-boot SD init is flaky - retry loop (43b: 5x, 200ms); skip macOS ._* dotfiles in the FatFS enumerator (PIZERO-10). SDIO mode (extra data pins GPIO37/38) is a stretch goal only if SPI throughput becomes a real problem (it didn't on prior ports). Acceptance: list files and read a ROM image from a FAT32 card.

summary: TLV320DAC3100 audio DAC bring-up: I2C config, I2S out, tone test
description: First target board with real audio hardware. TLV320DAC3100 configured over I2C (Stemma/I2C0 bus? verify address + bus in FRUITJAM-01/schematic), fed over I2S: DIN=24, MCLK=25 (optional - DAC PLL can run from BCLK), BCLK=26, WS=27, IRQ=23. CRITICAL sequencing: reset pin GPIO22 is SHARED with the ESP32-C6 reset - guide warns the C6 must be set up first, and toggling the shared reset clears any prior DAC setup; for the port we likely just hold C6 in a benign state and reset once before DAC config. I2S output from RP2350: PIO I2S or (if toolchain allows) HSTX is taken by video, so PIO or dedicated PIO block - plan PIO allocation across USB(1 PIO) + I2S. Outputs: 3.5mm stereo headphone + JST-SH mono speaker (class-D can add noise on headphone when both active - guide p.21). Acceptance: clean 48 kHz test tone on headphone jack and speaker.

summary: PSRAM bring-up: 8MB QMI init + usage policy (cold/bulk only)
description: 8 MB PSRAM populated (first port board with usable PSRAM; 43b had it but benched it). Init: QMI direct-mode setup (earlephilhower core has PSRAM support built in if that toolchain is chosen; else adapt 43b lib/psramlib TLSF allocator - it exists because the Pico SDK doesn't expose the needed QMI commands). POLICY per porting-rp2350.md Rule 3: PSRAM is poison in the per-frame hot path (the 43b's single biggest perf jump - 4.3x, COCO-42 - was removing a PSRAM framebuffer memcpy from an ISR). Use for cold/bulk only: ROM library cache, cassette/disk images, snapshots. CoCo RAM + framebuffers + hot code stay in SRAM. Acceptance: memtest passes over 8MB; policy documented in repo.

summary: Vendor XRoar 1.11 core (fresh extraction from upstream, not from prior port repos)
description: Extract the emulation core from upstream ~/github/xroar at the 1.11 release tag - deliberately NOT copying the drifted coco_machine/core copies in the three port repos (they diverged from each other; user directive: prior ports are insight, not source). Scope: mc6809, mc6883 (SAM), mc6821 (PIA), mc6847 (VDG), events, minimal portalib subset (rely on -ffunction-sections + --gc-sections instead of hand-stripping - amoled AMOLED-40 lesson). Apply the two universal unlocks from day one: (1) HOT_FUNC section attrs (hot.h pattern) on the dispatch/SAM/PIA/VDG hot set - ~20% (don't spray wider, there's a knee - AMOLED-31); (2) -DSUPPRESS_RENDER_SCANLINE no-oping per-scanline render while KEEPING HS/FS timing events (BASIC's IRQ timer needs them), frame-batched palette-LUT renderer instead (~2x - COCO-47/AMOLED-57). Beware bool-macro/delegate_void_bool token-pasting hazard (why pico.h can't enter portalib). Document provenance: PROVENANCE.md + THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md per pizero convention. Acceptance: core compiles for RP2350 and passes a headless smoke test (execute N 6809 cycles of Color BASIC ROM).

summary: Boot Color BASIC: machine glue, ROMs from SD, indexed frame to HSTX display, frame pacing
description: The integration milestone: CoCo 1/2 machine config wired to the vendored core (FRUITJAM-09), ROMs loaded from SD (FRUITJAM-06), VDG frame-batched renderer producing an indexed/palette frame handed to the HSTX consumer (FRUITJAM-04). The handoff contract per porting-rp2350.md Rule 4: core produces indexed frame + vsync/dirty signal; platform consumes per its hardware. Frame pacing per Rule 5: 15000 6809 cycles per 16762us frame (~0.895 MHz authentic), deadline spin with resync-not-debt on overrun, emulation rate decoupled from the 60 Hz display. Keep CoCo RAM in SRAM (XIP cache pollution - COCO-41). Acceptance: green screen, (C) TANDY / OK prompt at authentic speed.

summary: Dual-core split: core 0 emulation, core 1 presentation
description: Follow the proven pattern (pizero/amoled): core 0 = pure emulation; core 1 = display scanout callbacks + (later) audio island/I2S feed + SD service. HSTX scanout itself is near-zero CPU (hardware serializer + DMA), so core 1 has more headroom here than on any prior board. Handoff: RP2350 SRAM is cache-coherent across cores - volatile front-buffer pointer swap (pizero) or dirty flag (amoled), no mutexes. Decide after FRUITJAM-04 chooses the scanout library (pico_hdmi's scanline callback runs on whichever core calls video init - verify). Acceptance: emulation timing unaffected by display activity; measured headroom documented.

summary: USB HID keyboard to CoCo keyboard matrix mapping
description: Map TinyUSB HID boot-protocol reports to the CoCo PIA0 keyboard matrix in the vendored core. Port the MAPPING KNOWLEDGE from pizero (which keys go where, shift/ctrl handling, rollover behavior, BREAK=Escape etc.) but implement fresh against our core (user directive: no code copying). Includes key-repeat policy and a safe panic/reset chord. Depends: FRUITJAM-05 (reports arriving), FRUITJAM-10 (running machine). Acceptance: type a BASIC program on a real keyboard; all CoCo keys reachable.

summary: CoCo audio to the TLV320 DAC: PIA tap, 48 kHz ring, I2S sink
description: The audio seam, first-class on this board. Source side (pattern proven on pizero, implement fresh): tap PIA1 port A 6-bit DAC + single-bit sound (single-bit source is PIA1 CB2, not PB1 - pizero correction), event-driven integrate-and-dump resampler keyed to CPU cycle timestamps, emit 48 kHz mono into a power-of-two SPSC ring (pizero needed 8192 deep, primed to half, to survive fast-forward - PIZERO-39); hold-last on underrun. Sink side: core 1 feeds the ring to I2S/TLV320 (FRUITJAM-07) - a far simpler consumer than pizero's HDMI islands, no per-scanline metering, just steady I2S DMA. Note upstream-xroar issue 11 (bare-metal audio seam design) - this port is its first real I2S instantiation; feed findings back. Known open quality item: pizero's residual source-side fidelity gap vs desktop XRoar (PIZERO-41, open) - watch whether our fresh tap reproduces it. Acceptance: BASIC SOUND/PLAY commands audibly correct on headphones + speaker.

summary: Optional: HDMI audio via HSTX data islands (second audio sink)
description: Stretch goal after FRUITJAM-13 works. Sound over the DVI/HDMI cable using data islands on the HSTX path. Prior art: pico_hdmi has TERC4 data-island audio built in (48 kHz stereo, lock-free packet queue) - if FRUITJAM-04 adopts pico_hdmi this may be nearly free; pizero's dvi_data_island.c encoder (Takano pico_lib, MIT) is the portable packet layer if we need our own (its libdvi DMA-repoint injection layer does NOT carry to HSTX). Sink caveats from pizero: some monitors ignore ACR (pitch rides the video clock); 'DVI' sinks may not do audio at all - which is exactly why the DAC (FRUITJAM-13) is the primary path. Acceptance: audio over the HDMI cable on at least one monitor, selectable vs/alongside DAC output.

summary: Performance: lock 100% real-time with headroom; keep a perf log
description: Target: authentic real-time CoCo (~0.895 MHz 6809) locked, with measured headroom for audio+USB. The playbook says the architecture (not the clock) does the work: HOT_FUNC hot set in SRAM (+20%), CoCo RAM in SRAM, frame-batched render (~2x), PSRAM out of hot path, then clock headroom last. Known non-wins to NOT chase (all measured flat on amoled): hand-devirtualizing mem_cycle without inlining (AMOLED-56), manual portalib stripping (AMOLED-40), spraying HOT_FUNC beyond the core set (AMOLED-31). HSTX should make this the easiest board yet: display scanout ~0 CPU vs 43b's ISR streaming or pizero's libdvi TMDS. Keep docs/perf-log.md from first boot (43b convention - its 11%->99.9%-locked trajectory was the most useful porting record). Acceptance: sustained 100% real-time with headroom numbers documented.

summary: Board UI: buttons + NeoPixels
description: Button 1 = GPIO0 (also BOOT at power-on - avoid overloading in ways that trap users in bootloader), Button 2 = GPIO4, Button 3 = GPIO5. Candidate functions: machine reset, cassette/disk picker, display/audio toggles. 5 NeoPixels on GPIO32 as status (power/SD/USB/emulation-health); red LED GPIO29 is ACTIVE-LOW and shared with the IR receiver - probably leave it alone. Low priority polish; design after the milestone boots.

summary: USB hot-replug: implement the detect-and-restart fix
description: Implement the fix DESIGNED on pizero but never built (PIZERO-11b, diagnosis commit 4d47ae5): on rev-3 silicon the PIO-USB TX SM pins D+/D- at J after enumeration, so unplug is electrically invisible and manifests as a ~180/s flood of byte-identical HID reports. Fix: in tuh_hid_report_received_cb, detect >=N consecutive byte-identical reports, then pio_usb_host_stop()/restart to force re-enumeration. IMPORTANT twist on this board: the CH334F hub is always attached - the hub may absorb/report port changes properly, making replug just work (or fail differently). Test hub behavior FIRST (part of FRUITJAM-05 acceptance notes) before assuming pizero's failure mode. If we build it, offer the fix back to the pizero repo.

summary: Joystick via USB HID gamepad
description: Map a USB HID gamepad (via the hub) to CoCo analog joystick axes + fire button (PIA/SAM sampling in the core). Open on every prior port (PIZERO-13 never done) - this board could be the first to land it; the two USB-A ports make keyboard+gamepad simultaneously practical (hub permitting, USB 1.1 gamepads only). Depends: FRUITJAM-05/10/12.

summary: Cassette and disk images from SD
description: Beyond bare ROM loading: cassette images (.cas, maybe .wav) via the core's tape interface, and - stretch - DOS cartridge + WD279x + .dsk support (heavier: adds disk controller emulation + more ROMs). Sample-SD layout + AUTORUN convention like pizero's sample-sd/AUTORUN.md. PSRAM (FRUITJAM-08) is the natural home for loaded images. Scope decision (cassette-only vs disk too) once FRUITJAM-10 milestone is real.

summary: Docs: filled pre-port matrix, perf log, build/flash instructions, SD layout
description: Keep the repo's documentation current as the port progresses: the filled-in pre-port assessment matrix (porting-rp2350.md Step 1-2 for this board) in docs/, docs/perf-log.md (FRUITJAM-15), build + flash instructions for the chosen toolchain (FRUITJAM-02), SD card layout + ROM sourcing note, README status updates at each milestone. Explicitly out of scope for the port (documented as such): ESP32-C6 WiFi, IR receiver, Stemma QT peripherals.

summary: Adopt baseline contribution policy (CONTRIBUTING.md)
description: Stamp the baseline contribution policy into the repo as CONTRIBUTING.md (issue-first workflow, docs-as-source-of-truth, verify-don't-invent, bounded autonomy, tested-before-done). Filed retroactively per the contributing skill's bootstrap exception: the issues.jsonl tracker already existed (FRUITJAM-01..20) so this is not a genesis moment, but the policy adoption still gets its own issue. Left open until the maintainer reviews CONTRIBUTING.md and rubber-stamps it.

summary: CoCo 1/2 machine glue: memory map + SAM + PIA + VDG-sync IRQ (headless boot)
description: Hand-write the CoCo machine that wires the vendored chips (mc6809 CPU, mc6883 SAM, mc6821 PIA0/PIA1, ram, ROM) - machine.c/dragon.c was deliberately NOT vendored (FRUITJAM-09), so this is the machine we own. Memory map: 32K RAM $0000-$7FFF, Color BASIC ROM (bas12.rom, 8K) at $A000-$BFFF, PIA I/O $FF00-$FF5F, SAM control regs $FFC0-$FFDF, reset/vector page from ROM. Route the CPU mem_cycle delegate through SAM decode -> RAM/ROM/PIA. Wire PIA0 (keyboard matrix - return no-key so the scan loop idles; HS/FS sync inputs on CA1/CB1) and PIA1 (VDG mode bits on PB, 6-bit DAC + single-bit sound on PA/CB2, cassette) with the minimal delegates needed to not hang. Drive CPU IRQ from PIA0's interrupt output, fed by VDG horizontal/field-sync events (keep these events live even with per-scanline render suppressed). Keep CoCo RAM in SRAM (COCO-41). Follow the upstream tag-1.11 wiring faithfully (dragon.c). Acceptance (headless, serial-verified): CPU resets to the ROM vector and executes Color BASIC into its keyboard-poll idle loop; PC sits in the poll region and the 60 Hz IRQ is ticking (visible via an IRQ counter / expected screen-RAM init to $60 spaces).

summary: Frame pacing: authentic ~0.895 MHz run loop, resync-not-debt, decoupled from 60 Hz display
description: The emulation timing loop per porting-rp2350.md Rule 5: run 15000 6809 cycles per 16762 us NTSC frame (~0.895 MHz authentic), deadline-spin to the frame boundary, and RESYNC (not accumulate debt) on overrun so a slow frame can't spiral. Emulation rate is decoupled from the 60 Hz display refresh (display consumes latest frame independently). Builds on FRUITJAM-22's machine. Depends on the SAM MPU-rate handling for cycle counting. Acceptance (headless, serial-verified): sustained real-time rate measured at ~1.0x (0.895 MHz) with the pacing loop; overrun injection shows resync (no runaway debt); report measured headroom (cycles/frame budget vs consumed).

summary: VDG frame-batched indexed renderer: CoCo video RAM -> palette/indexed frame + vsync signal
description: Implement the frame-batched palette-LUT renderer (the -DSUPPRESS_RENDER_SCANLINE approach from FRUITJAM-09: no-op per-scanline pixel render, KEEP HS/FS timing events): once per field, read CoCo video RAM through the SAM video-address logic and produce an indexed/palette frame + a vsync/dirty signal per the Rule 4 handoff contract (core produces indexed frame; platform consumes). Cover the modes the BASIC prompt uses first: VDG alphanumeric (text, incl. inverse/semigraphics-4 chars from the internal font) then semigraphics. Palette = CoCo VDG colors (green-screen text is the key case). Depends FRUITJAM-22. Acceptance (host/serial-checkable): a rendered CoCo text field where the expected cells are present - e.g. the '(C) TANDY' / 'OK' glyph codes appear at the right screen-RAM offsets in the produced indexed frame.

summary: HSTX integration + green screen: indexed->RGB565 centered, ROMs from SD, on-hardware boot
description: The milestone payoff: tie FRUITJAM-22/23/24 to the display (FRUITJAM-04) and SD (FRUITJAM-06). Convert the VDG indexed/palette frame to the display's 320x240 RGB565 framebuffer, centering the CoCo 256x192 active area (pizero-proven scale) - written into the pico_hdmi scanline path (g_fb + scanline_cb). Load bas12.rom from 0:/coco/roms/ via FatFs at boot. Add the integration build env. This is the first end-to-end run on real hardware. Acceptance: on the monitor over DVI - CoCo green screen with the '(C) TANDY' copyright line and blinking 'OK' cursor at authentic speed (Color BASIC alone; Extended not required for the milestone).
